title: Smooth additive specialist (EBM/GAM) blended with tree model
summary: Your current top solutions are heavily tree-plus-hand-feature oriented.
rationale: Your current top solutions are heavily tree-plus-hand-feature oriented. An Explainable Boosting Machine or boosted GAM can learn smooth monotone-ish response shapes for `TyreLife`, `RaceProgress`, `LapsRemaining_Est`, and degradation features, then contribute a meaningfully different error pattern to an ensemble.
implementation_hint: Train an EBM/GAM specialist on a compact feature set emphasizing continuous wear/progress variables plus a few low-order interactions (`TyreLife×Compound`, `TyreLife×RaceProgress`, `LapTime_Delta×Compound`). Use its OOF predictions as one extra meta-feature or rank-average it with the best tree model. Inspect learned shapes to prune obviously spurious engineered features.
expected_effect: Diversity gain rather than solo-model gain; can stabilize oversharp tree predictions and sometimes improve AUC through complementary ordering on boundary cases.
risk: If the signal is dominated by high-order race-specific interactions, the additive specialist may underfit and contribute little. Blending must be OOF to avoid optimistic CV.
